title: PT Study Tutor Entry Card & Canvas Fixes
description: Fix the three blocking UI bugs in the /tutor Studio workspace entry card and panel centering flow.

userStories.title: Entry card renders as viewport overlay, not canvas object
userStories.description: The 'Start A Fresh Study Session' entry card currently renders inside the TransformComponent canvas div, so it gets panned/zoomed with the canvas and appears at canvas center (2000,2000) instead of screen center. Move the entry card OUTSIDE the TransformComponent and position it as a viewport overlay (absolute to the viewport wrapper, not the canvas). It should always appear centered on the user's screen regardless of canvas pan/zoom state.
acceptanceCriteria: Entry card is centered in the visible viewport, not the canvas
Entry card does NOT move when the canvas is panned or zoomed
Entry card boundingBox.y is in the upper third of the viewport (not at 2000px)
dev-browser verification: navigate to /tutor, check entry card boundingBox is within viewport

userStories.title: Material picker shows filenames not full paths
userStories.description: In the entry card material picker, each material checkbox label shows the full source_path like 'C:\Users\treyt\Documents\PT\Week9\Chapter10.pdf' instead of just 'Chapter10.pdf'. Extract just the filename from material.source_path or material.title. Use: label = material.title || material.source_path.split(/[\\/]/).pop() || 'Unknown material'
acceptanceCriteria: Material labels show only the filename, not the full path
Material labels do NOT contain backslashes or colons
Labels still show file type badge (PDF, MP4, etc.)
dev-browser verification: check that no material label text contains '\' or ':'

userStories.title: Auto-center panels after Start Priming opens them
userStories.description: When the user clicks Start Priming and panels open via the priming preset, the viewport does NOT auto-frame the newly opened panels. The user sees empty canvas or partially visible panels. After handleStartPrimingFromEntry opens the priming preset, trigger focusOpenPanels() or centerOpenPanels() with a short delay (requestAnimationFrame or setTimeout 300ms) to auto-frame the newly opened panels in the viewport.
acceptanceCriteria: After clicking Start Priming, the viewport auto-pans to show the opened panels
At least one panel has a boundingBox that intersects the visible viewport
Panels are not cut off at edges — they are framed with padding
dev-browser verification: click Start Priming, wait 2s, check panel visibility in viewport
